title: First World Championship race
summary: The first F1 World Championship race was at Silverstone in 1950.
detail: Giuseppe Farina won the inaugural Formula One World Championship race on May 13, 1950, at Silverstone in the United Kingdom. Only seven races counted toward that first title.

title: Youngest world champion
summary: Max Verstappen became champion at 24 years old in 2021.
detail: Max Verstappen clinched his first drivers' title at the 2021 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix, becoming one of the youngest champions in F1 history after a dramatic season finale.

title: Monaco's tight streets
summary: The Monaco Grand Prix is the slowest and most glamorous race on the calendar.
detail: Monaco's street circuit has barely any room for error — barriers line the entire track. Overtaking is extremely difficult, so qualifying position is especially important.

title: Points for top ten
summary: Finishing 10th still earns 1 championship point.
detail: The current points system awards 25 for a win, 18 for second, down to 1 point for tenth place. Both driver and constructor championships use the same points.

title: Safety Car
summary: A Safety Car bunches the field together after a big incident.
detail: When track conditions are too dangerous, a Safety Car leads the field at reduced speed. Racing resumes when the marshals clear the incident and the SC pulls into the pits.

title: DRS overtaking boost
summary: DRS opens a flap on the rear wing to reduce drag and help passing.
detail: Drivers can activate DRS when they're within one second of the car ahead on designated straights. It gives a temporary speed advantage to encourage overtaking.

title: Pit stops in under 3 seconds
summary: Top teams change all four tyres in about two seconds.
detail: A pit crew of around 20 people swaps tyres and adjusts the car during a race. One slow stop can cost several positions on track.

title: Most titles — drivers
summary: Lewis Hamilton and Michael Schumacher share the record with 7 titles each.
wikipediaTitle: List of Formula One World Drivers' Champions
detail: Michael Schumacher won five consecutive titles with Ferrari from 2000–2004. Lewis Hamilton matched his seven championships with McLaren and Mercedes.

title: Ferrari — oldest team
summary: Scuderia Ferrari is the only team to have competed in every F1 season since 1950.
detail: Enzo Ferrari founded the team that bears his name. Ferrari is the most successful constructor in F1 history by total race wins and championships.

title: Sprint race weekends
summary: On sprint weekends, Saturday's sprint sets part of Sunday's grid.
detail: Sprint weekends shorten traditional practice and add a 100 km race on Saturday. Sprint results decide the starting order for Sunday's main Grand Prix.

title: Halo head protection
summary: The halo titanium ring has saved drivers from serious injuries.
detail: Introduced in 2018, the halo protects a driver's head from impacts with barriers, debris, or other cars. It was controversial at first but is now credited with preventing fatalities.

title: Night race in Singapore
summary: Singapore hosted F1's first modern night race in 2008.
detail: Floodlights illuminate the Marina Bay street circuit. The heat and humidity make it one of the most physically demanding races for drivers.

title: Tyre compounds
summary: Teams must use at least two different tyre compounds during a dry race.
detail: Pirelli supplies soft, medium, and hard compounds (plus intermediates and wets for rain). Softer tyres are faster but wear out quicker.

title: Chequered flag
summary: The black-and-white flag means the race is over.
detail: The chequered flag has ended motor races for over a century. In F1, the first car to cross the line after seeing it wins the Grand Prix.

title: 2026 power unit rules
summary: 2026 brings new engines with more electric power and sustainable fuel.
detail: The 2026 regulations increase hybrid electric contribution and require 100% sustainable fuel. Cars are also slightly shorter and lighter to improve racing.

title: Constructors matter
summary: Teams score points too — the Constructors' Championship is a separate title.
detail: Both drivers' points count toward their team total. The Constructors' title brings prestige and a larger share of prize money to the team.

title: Fastest lap bonus
summary: The driver with the fastest lap gets an extra point — if they finish in the top ten.
detail: Since 2019, one bonus point goes to the fastest lap finisher inside the top ten. It can decide tight championship battles late in the season.

title: National anthems
summary: The winner's and their team's anthems play on the podium.
detail: After each race, the top three drivers stand on the podium. The winning driver's national anthem plays first, followed by their team's anthem.

title: Longest race distance
summary: Every race must be at least 305 km (about 190 miles), except Monaco.
detail: Most races run for roughly 90 minutes or 305 km, whichever comes first. Monaco is shorter because its lap is only 3.3 km long.

title: Racing around the world
summary: The F1 calendar visits five continents and over 20 countries.
detail: From Melbourne to Abu Dhabi, teams fly cars, equipment, and hundreds of staff to each race. The season runs from March to December.
